title: Lightning Strikes the Platform
description: On pages 17–20, lightning strikes the platform focuses on Luffy, Buggy, Alvida, Smoker as buggy captures Luffy at the place where Gold Roger died, turning Loguetown into a crisis.
scene: On pages 17–20, lightning strikes the platform focuses on Luffy, Buggy, Alvida, Smoker as buggy captures Luffy at the place where Gold Roger died, turning Loguetown into a crisis.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Where did they run off to?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: They're headed for the Western harbour.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: One of our squads should be there by now.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Well, that's Umm... the sudden rain has rendered their gunpowder useless,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: So they headed back to the station to resupply...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: So the Harbour is completely clear!?

dialogue.speaker: Dragon
dialogue.text: The winds are blowing west... so if they were to set sail, they'd catch the tailwind.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Is all this purely a council- dance...!?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: It's as if the heavens them- selves want that boy to survive!

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: On the pride of a marine headquarters captain, I, smoker the white hunter,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Wow to not allow that boy to escape this island!

summary: This part turns Loguetown into a trap, bringing old enemies and Marine pressure together around Luffy.
